title: Cochlospermum potentilloides
intro: Cochlospermum potentilloides is a tropical plant species in the family Cochlospermaceae, commonly found in the regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cochlospermum potentilloides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Cochlospermum potentilloides is a species in the family Cochlospermaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cochlospermum family. This family belongs to the order Malvales, which includes other flowering plant families such as the mallow and hibiscus families. The order Malvales is part of the clade Rosids, a large group of flowering plants that also includes the rose and apple families. The clade Rosids is part of the larger clade of flowering plants, the eudicots, which is characterized by having four or five petals. Within the eudicots, the clade Rosids is one of the two main groups, the other being the asterids.
